artifact: Clinical Intelligence Stack global target map v0.1
status: source anchored distribution and audience map
public_hook: Medical AI needs a clinical intelligence stack, not another chatbot benchmark.
distribution_rule: Do not cold pitch a partnership. Publish a clear artifact, then point each audience to the part that solves their technical problem.

source_anchors.name: DeepSeek R1
source_anchors.supported_signal: Reasoning model work values reinforcement learning, verification, self reflection, and dynamic strategy adaptation.
source_anchors.target_angle: Clinical reasoning needs verifier rows that capture state, time, missing variables, and source support.

source_anchors.name: OpenAI HealthBench
source_anchors.supported_signal: HealthBench uses realistic health conversations and physician created rubrics.
source_anchors.target_angle: Clinical Intelligence Stack extends the same direction into state language and trajectories.

source_anchors.name: Stanford MedHELM
source_anchors.supported_signal: MedHELM maps medical model evaluation across a clinician validated taxonomy and clinical tasks.
source_anchors.target_angle: Clinical Intelligence Stack can add a state and trajectory layer to task taxonomies.

source_anchors.name: AgentClinic
source_anchors.supported_signal: AgentClinic evaluates simulated clinical environments with interaction, incomplete information, tools, specialties, and languages.
source_anchors.target_angle: Clinical Intelligence Stack gives agentic medicine a compact patient state and verifier language.

source_anchors.name: Google MedGemma
source_anchors.supported_signal: MedGemma requires validation for the developer intended use case and may require adaptation.
source_anchors.target_angle: Clinical Intelligence Stack offers use case specific state, trajectory, and verifier artifacts.

source_anchors.name: SBSGM Yapay Zeka ve Yenilikçi Teknolojiler Dairesi
source_anchors.supported_signal: The official page defines duties around AI improvable processes, solution production, collaboration, and education materials.
source_anchors.target_angle: Clinical Intelligence Stack can be framed as public research infrastructure, not official collaboration.

source_anchors.name: TEKNOFEST Sağlıkta Yapay Zeka
source_anchors.supported_signal: The public competition route makes health AI teams visible in Türkiye.
source_anchors.target_angle: Clinical Intelligence Stack can supply language for safer project claims and trajectory based evaluation.

source_anchors.name: TÜYZE
source_anchors.supported_signal: TÜYZE is the visible national health data and AI institute surface.
source_anchors.target_angle: Clinical Intelligence Stack can speak to health data, AI, and clinical workflow representation without claiming institutional support.

audience_routes.why_they_care: They need verifiers beyond math and code. Clinical reasoning has state, time, missing data, and source support.
audience_routes.public_line: Reasoning models need clinical verifiers that track state, time, missing data, and source support.
audience_routes.first_channel: X thread after GitHub proof is visible

audience_routes.why_they_care: They already moved beyond exam style tasks toward realistic rubrics, task taxonomies, and simulated clinical environments.
audience_routes.artifact_to_show: Clinical State Language and Clinical Trajectory Engine
audience_routes.public_line: Medical evals need a patient state layer between a prompt and a score.
audience_routes.first_channel: LinkedIn technical post with repo link

examples: MedGemma and open health model developers
audience_routes.why_they_care: Use case specific validation and adaptation need concrete state and trajectory artifacts.
audience_routes.public_line: A developer model needs a clinical use case map before anyone can trust a smooth answer.
audience_routes.first_channel: GitHub README and public forum friendly post

audience_routes.why_they_care: Türkiye has visible AI and health AI routes. The missing public layer is clinical intelligence representation.
audience_routes.public_line: Türkiye can contribute clinical intelligence infrastructure, not only health AI applications.
audience_routes.first_channel: Turkish LinkedIn post after English technical thread

audience_routes.audience: AI hardware and national technology circles
examples: Baykar and T3 AI adjacent circles
audience_routes.why_they_care: National AI visibility needs hard technical artifacts with health relevance, not vague calls for innovation.
audience_routes.public_line: Clinical AI infrastructure is a national technology problem when it touches language, data, agents, and care workflows.
audience_routes.first_channel: short Turkish post with one architecture visual

launch_sequence.name: technical proof post
launch_sequence.goal: show GitHub proof before asking for attention
launch_sequence.gate: repo link live and PR body aligned

launch_sequence.name: clinician builder post
launch_sequence.goal: explain why a physician is building state language and trajectories

launch_sequence.name: Turkiye public frame
launch_sequence.goal: frame Türkiye as a contributor to clinical intelligence infrastructure
launch_sequence.gate: no official support or partnership claim

launch_sequence.name: targeted public replies
launch_sequence.goal: join relevant public discussions with one concrete artifact
launch_sequence.surface: public comments only after live profile and thread check
launch_sequence.gate: no cold claim, no private data, no endorsement language

blocked_moves: private email to public figures without a live reason
claiming guaranteed reposts, likes, or approval
next_artifacts: public reply snippets for model teams and health benchmark builders
